what_input_feature: After font/cmap parsing routes the buffer to HB_SCRIPT_GUJARATI, the current glyph's codepoint (a 32-bit value compared inside a switch) must equal the literal 0x0A85u. The CMP at line 172 is `buffer->cur().codepoint == 0x0A85u`; loser inputs do reach the Gujarati per-glyph loop (line 167 F=0 W=45, F=0 L=150) but their codepoints fall through to the default arm.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning seeds contain the literal little-endian bytes `85 0a` adjacent in the payload (e.g. seed 07e89a36cf4fe8ee begins `85 0a 00 00`, and seed 33370b3cd9a8dde4 has `85 0a` at offset 0x2a). At line 172 the W overlay shows T=3 F=42 — the true branch fires only under W, confirming a codepoint==0x0A85 outcome.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser inputs reach HB_SCRIPT_GUJARATI (line 166 T=10 F=0 under L) and iterate the loop 150 times (line 167 T=150) but never produce codepoint 0x0A85 — at line 172 L shows T=0 F=150, so every glyph falls through to the 0x0AC5 arm or default. None of the five loser seeds carry the byte pair `85 0a` together.
mechanism_attribution: The decisive delta between vpc (winner) and cmplog (loser) is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP gradient bucket. cmplog has the input-to-state mutator and naive havoc but no Hamming/prefix-distance feedback, so it gets no gradient credit for getting closer to 0x0A85; vpc has the same comparison-substitution kit plus CMP_MAP, so every approach toward the constant (e.g. flipping one byte from 0x0B to 0x0A or from 0x84 to 0x85) is rewarded as new feedback and survives in the corpus. The mechanism enabling vpc here is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback signal, not input-to-state byte substitution.

what_input_feature: The byte at PNG file offset 0x18 (the IHDR bit_depth field) must be >= 16, with 0x10 (decimal 16) being the canonical winning value. The byte must reach pngset.c via a parsed IHDR chunk so info_ptr->bit_depth carries that value at the tRNS-handling site.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 trials and the one winner-resolving seed carries 0x10 at offset 0x18 — exactly the threshold value 16 that flips `info_ptr->bit_depth < 16` to false. CMP_MAP/value-profile feedback rewards inputs whose bit_depth field byte moves toward 16 along the 1-byte CMP gradient, so vpc gets a continuous signal during the climb from typical values (0x01/0x08) to 0x10.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 trials and blocks 9/10. Its loser seeds carry 0x08 or 0x01 at offset 0x18, both <16. cmplog has I2S but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient: I2SRandReplace can record CMP(bit_depth, 16) and try byte substitution, but with 8-bit operands the I2S substitution offset is essentially random across the input — without the value-profile distance reward there is no monotone signal to anchor the substitution at offset 0x18 specifically, so resolution depends on luck.
mechanism_attribution: The technique delta between value_profile_cmplog and cmplog is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP gradient. For the 1-byte comparison `info_ptr->bit_depth < 16` the CMP_MAP records the Hamming/distance between the runtime operand and the constant 16, giving vpc a smooth gradient signal that selects intermediate seeds whose bit_depth byte is closer to 16; this reliably converges to 0x10. cmplog has no such gradient — its I2S substitution is unguided about which offset to overwrite, which explains the 10/10 vs 1/10 split.

what_input_feature: An XML document body that exercises the entity/character-reference parsing path deeply enough to invoke xmlFatalErr with the specific enum constant XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF — i.e. an input containing structures like '<?xml' declarations, DTDs, or entity references that lead the parser to scan a malformed hex character reference. The blocking branch is the integer-switch arm 'case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:' on the runtime value of the parameter 'error' passed by an upstream caller; the input must drive a caller path that passes that exact enum value.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that bring intermediate-stage CMP operands (e.g. the byte-by-byte comparisons inside xmlIsNameChar, xmlParseNCName, xmlParseStartTag2, xmlParseCharData, xmlParseCharDataComplex) closer to their target tokens, letting the corpus climb the multi-step XML parsing pipeline and reach a caller that fires XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF. The HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE table shows W-side invocations of these upstream parsing functions dominate L by 10x–70x (e.g. xmlIsNameChar 560 vs 0; xmlParseNCName 401 vs 29), confirming the winner navigates deeper into the parser. value_profile_cmplog resolved 10/10 trials with avg duration 4.65h.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has input-to-state substitution (which can substitute the integer constant XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF directly into the input where its bytes appear in a CMP buffer) but lacks the CMP_MAP gradient. Without the gradient feedback on the many intermediate string/byte comparisons in the upstream parsing chain, cmplog only stochastically navigates to the caller that produces this specific error enum — it resolved only 1/10 trials (in trial 9, after 426s of mutations), while value_profile_cmplog resolved every trial.
mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient. The technique difference between winner (value_profile_cmplog) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile — i.e., the CMP_MAP/Hamming-similarity feedback. Both fuzzers carry input-to-state substitution, so the integer switch-case substitution capability is shared; what cmplog lacks is the upstream gradient signal that rewards inputs incrementally approaching deeper parsing states. The 10x–70x W/L hit-count divergence on upstream NameChar/NCName/StartTag2/CharData functions is the direct fingerprint of CMP_MAP guiding corpus growth along the parsing chain that culminates in firing XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF.

what_input_feature: The first four bytes of the input must equal the 32-bit little-endian encoding of codepoint 0x0D91 (i.e., 0x91 0x0D 0x00 0x00). After a single decoding hop into buffer->cur().codepoint, the Sinhala switch at line 309 then takes the case 0x0D91u arm (true side of the case-label branch).
why_winner_satisfies: Every value_profile winner-resolving seed shown begins with the exact 4-byte little-endian encoding of 0x0D91 (91 0d 00 00); the BYTE DIFF section confirms offset 0x0000 is byte 0x91 in 8/8 W seeds and offset 0x0001 is byte 0x0d in 8/8 W seeds, while loser seeds show those bytes only sporadically.
why_loser_doesnt: naive uses only edge-counter feedback with havoc/token mutators; it has no signal to grind a 4-byte integer toward equality with the literal 0x0D91, so the loser seeds rarely produce the magic 4-byte prefix and instead carry unrelated 16-bit/32-bit values at offsets 0x00-0x03 (e.g. 00 01 00 00, 6a 0c ff 00, af 93 76 af).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. Each integer CMP that compares buffer->cur().codepoint against a switch-case literal (0x0D85, 0x0D8B, ..., 0x0D91, ...) writes a Hamming-distance bucket into CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, distance). An input whose first 32-bit word is numerically closer to 0x0D91 than any previously-seen input adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved — giving value_profile a monotone gradient toward the exact match. naive lacks any CMP feedback and must rely on havoc luck to set 4 specific bytes simultaneously.

what_input_feature: A well-formed DOCTYPE prologue followed by an internal-subset opener: the byte stream must traverse the xmlParseLookupInternalSubset state machine through state=0 -> state='"' (via *cur=='"' at line 11304) -> back to state=0 -> *cur=='[' (also at line 11304, sets state=']') so that the blocker `else if (state == ']')` evaluates true. Equivalently, the input must contain the literal sequence `<!DOCTYPE <name> ... "..." ... [` reaching the state-machine's bracket-tracking arm.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile reaches this branch on every trial (resolved=10/10) and the per-role overlay shows it executing lines 11269-11272 (state=0; start=cur; continue;) exclusively — meaning value_profile actually enters the state==']' arm, encounters a non-']' non-'>' non-blank byte, and continues the scan. At the L11262 sub-branch the winner has T=0 F=24, i.e. it entered the state==']' block 24 times.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 trials and is unreached on 2/10. Its hitcount on the blocker is 0. The overlay shows lines 11263-11265 (the L-only success-path of state==']' && *cur=='>') reached only by loser-blocking seeds — i.e. the few times the loser enters the block it immediately exits via return(1), while never producing the broader exploration that the winner does. naive lacks any CMP-distance signal so it cannot build a gradient through the chained single-byte character comparisons (*cur=='<', '"', '\'', ']', '>', etc.) that gate every state transition; without that gradient, it spends 6.31h on average blocked here vs the winner's 1.10h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a (PC, Hamming-distance-bucket) entry on every intercepted character-equality CMP. Each distinct byte at *cur produces a new bucket at PCs like L11262 (*cur=='>'), L11267 (IS_BLANK_CH), L11269 (*cur!=']'), L11293 (*cur=='<'), L11304 (*cur=='"' || '\'' || ']'). This per-byte feedback rewards inputs that drive each gate one step closer to the target character, building up the multi-step DOCTYPE/internal-subset grammar that naive cannot stumble onto with edge-only feedback. cmplog (I2S substitution) helps less here because each gate is a 1-byte literal comparison rather than a multi-byte magic — there is little to substitute, but the CMP_MAP buckets still reward proximity.

what_input_feature: A literal ']' byte (0x5D) must appear at a cur position while the state machine is in state==0 (outside a quoted string, not inside a comment). Reaching this gate's true side specifically requires the third disjunct `*cur == ']'` to match — i.e. byte 0x5D at the scanned offset.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile's CMP_MAP records a Hamming-distance bucket for the integer compare `*cur == ']'`. Inputs whose byte at the cur offset gets bit-closer to 0x5D enter a new CMP_MAP bucket and are preserved as corpus, providing a gradient that culminates in an actual ']' byte landing at the right position. Winner saw avg hitcount=9 (true side fired ~9 times per trial).
why_loser_doesnt: naive has only edge-counter feedback — the integer comparison `*cur == ']'` is just one edge that either fires the true side or doesn't, with no partial-match gradient. Without CMP_MAP, naive never gets a reward signal for bytes that are close-but-not-equal to 0x5D, so it must stumble onto a 0x5D placement by chance and never does within 10 trials (loser blocked=8/10, resolved=0/10, hitcount=0).
mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient feedback in value_profile's CMP-interception instrumentation rewards Hamming-distance progress toward the literal 0x5D operand of the equality compare on line 11304, building a corpus gradient that naive's pure edge feedback cannot construct.

what_input_feature: The PNG IHDR color_type byte (file offset 0x19) must not carry bit 0x02 (PNG_COLOR_MASK_COLOR). Concretely color_type in {0, 3, 4}; the winner-resolving seeds use 0x00 (grayscale), while loser-blocking seeds use 0x06 (RGB+alpha). Correlated IHDR fields at 0x13 (width LSB), 0x18 (bit_depth), and 0x1c (interlace) must also remain self-consistent so the chunk reaches the gate.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds put 0x00 at offset 0x19 (grayscale color_type). The expression (color_type & 0x02) then evaluates to 0, so the gate at L1216 takes the false (winning) branch reaching the else arm at L1224-L1230.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds put 0x06 at offset 0x19 (RGB+alpha color_type). (0x06 & 0x02) = 0x02 != 0, so the gate at L1216 takes the true (blocked) branch into L1217-L1222. All 10 loser-blocking seeds have 0x06 at this offset; only color_type values lacking the 0x02 bit clear the gate.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that come closer to satisfying integer comparisons. The bitwise-AND-with-literal gate at L1216 emits a trace_cmp with one operand being (color_type & 0x02) and the other being 0; CMP_MAP-bucket feedback distinguishes color_type=0x06 (bit set) from color_type=0x00 (bit clear), so inputs flipping the 0x02 bit toward zero are preserved as corpus. naive and cmplog lack this gradient: naive has only edge feedback, and cmplog's input-to-state substitution mutator cannot meaningfully substitute a 1-bit bitmask literal as if it were a multi-byte magic constant. cmplog therefore performs no better than naive here (both are losers; pair shape BBRR), while value_profile_cmplog inherits the CMP_MAP gradient and resolves at the same rate as value_profile.

what_input_feature: Bytes that survive harfbuzz font parsing far enough to set buffer->props.script = HB_SCRIPT_MODI before reaching the switch at hb-ot-shaper-vowel-constraints.cc:56. The script field is populated by upstream font/cmap/feature processing from input bytes; the blocker case-arm fires only when that derived field equals the MODI constant.
why_winner_satisfies: Naive (Pair 1 winner) reaches MODI 10/10 trials via random havoc on a font-shaped corpus (winner seeds carry an OTTO TrueType magic header and a `df 20 20` payload that drives script selection toward MODI). value_profile_cmplog (Pair 2 winner) reaches MODI 9/10 trials because the added value-profile gradient feedback rewards seeds that get closer to MODI in upstream comparisons, escaping the corpus state cmplog gets trapped in.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks 8/10 trials because its input-to-state substitution mutator harvests the literal codepoint constants from the inner switches of OTHER scripts (DEVANAGARI 0x0905/0x0906/0x0909/0x090F/0x0930, TELUGU 0x0C12/0x0C3F/0x0C46/0x0C4A, MALAYALAM 0x0D07/0x0D09/0x0D0E/0x0D12, TAKRI 0x11680/0x11686). The loser overlay shows cmplog explores those competing case-arms heavily (15 traversals of each inner switch) but never lands on MODI: the input-to-state substitution pulls inputs toward non-MODI script values, polluting the corpus away from the blocker.
mechanism_attribution: Pattern B (input-to-state substitution hurts). The MODI case-arm is not a literal in the input — buffer->props.script is a derived field set by upstream font parsing. cmplog's input-to-state mutator finds many literal codepoints in the inner switch CMPs of other scripts and substitutes them, but those substitutions push the derived script field toward DEVANAGARI/TELUGU/MALAYALAM/TAKRI rather than MODI. naive avoids this trap by doing pure random byte mutations (no CMP-directed pollution), and vpc escapes it via the added value-profile gradient over upstream comparisons that lead to MODI selection.

what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG prelude (signature + IHDR) followed by an iTXt chunk whose 4-byte big-endian length header is set to a value large enough that the subsequent png_read_buffer(length+1, 1) call cannot allocate it (png_malloc_base returns NULL), forcing the True branch at pngrutil.c:2748. The input does not need to actually contain `length` bytes of chunk data — only the declared length field matters because the allocation is attempted before the data is read.
why_winner_satisfies: Both winners (naive and value_profile_cmplog) reach the buffer==NULL True branch in 8/10 trials. The winner-resolving seeds we have are 8-12 KB sized PNGs whose declared iTXt chunk length is large enough to defeat png_malloc_base; the per-role overlay shows W exclusively executing the OOM-handling block (lines 2749-2753) and never the buffer!=NULL post-buffer-read path, confirming buffer == NULL fires.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 trials and is blocked 9/10 with avg duration 10.80h. Its I2SRandReplace mutator, driven by CmpLogObserver entries on the chunk-length and chunk-type CMPs that fire during chunk-header parsing, systematically substitutes the length field back to plausible in-file values (or to constants seen in length CMPs upstream), pinning length to ranges that png_malloc_base can satisfy. The L seeds in BRANCH SEEDS take the False branch and continue into the prefix-scanning loop at L2761-L2764 and beyond — i.e., they reach the chunk with a length the allocator handled.
mechanism_attribution: Composite Pattern-B story (claimed_mechanism = other; no single positive enabler explains both decisive pairs). On (naive, cmplog) the input-to-state substitution mutator that cmplog carries on top of naive's stack is counterproductive here: it scans CmpLogObserver entries from chunk-length and chunk-type comparisons and pulls the 4-byte iTXt length field back toward plausible recorded values, undoing the random-large mutations that naive's pure havoc/token stack produces freely. On (value_profile_cmplog, cmplog) the value-profile Hamming-distance feedback signal rewards progress on the allocation-size comparison path independently of the input-to-state substitutions that trap cmplog. Both decisive pairs collapse to the same single feature requirement (a huge declared iTXt length), so the technique label `other` is the honest pick — it is the I2S sub-mutator hurting cmplog that drives the divergence, not any single positive enabler shared by the two winners.
